disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Rash is geometric, mapped exactly to the belt-buckle contact area, not a dermatomal band; no severe neuropathic prodromal pain (excludes herpes zoster)
trigger_question_examples: Does the rash match the exact shape of the buckle, or does it form a band across one side of your body?
Did you have burning nerve-type pain in a stripe before the rash?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It matches the buckle shape exactly and doesn't form a band; no nerve-type pain beforehand.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No spreading beyond the contact area with increasing pain, no honey-crusting/pustules, no fever (excludes secondary bacterial infection); no systemic anaphylaxis
trigger_question_examples: Is the redness spreading well beyond the buckle area and becoming painful, or oozing thick yellow pus, with a fever?
Any whole-body hives or breathing trouble?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, it's staying in the buckle area, no thick yellow pus or fever, and no whole-body hives or breathing trouble.
